macro_summary: Mega-cap AI names dominate overnight screening as markets brace for a dense two-week catalyst window: June CPI on July 14, TSMC earnings July 16, FOMC decision July 29, and a cluster of mega-cap prints (MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30). The 10-year yield sits at a 4-week high of 4.58% with markets pricing ~22–25% probability of a July hike, keeping the macro backdrop binary. AI infrastructure capex narratives remain the dominant fundamental driver, with hyperscalers collectively guiding toward $1T+ in 2027 spend, creating significant valuation dislocations in semiconductor and software names that appeared across all overnight screens.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold for inclusion: 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying ideas cleared at 3+ appearances; 3 ideas achieved perfect 6/6 consensus.
key_events: July 10 — SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) IPO debut on Nasdaq today; regular trading opens Monday July 13
July 14 — June CPI print at 8:30am ET (May was 4.2% YoY); pivotal pre-FOMC data point
July 16 — TSMC earnings; AI capex sector read-through for NVDA and semis
July 28–29 — FOMC rate decision (78% hold, ~22% hike probability)
July 29 — MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings after close; Azure growth and FY2027 capex guidance key
July 29 — New SKHY Korean shares enter KOSPI circulation; potential dilution/selling pressure
July 30 — AAPL fiscal Q3 2026 earnings; foldable iPhone buzz as additional optionality
August 26 — NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings after close

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 overnight screens with high conviction in 5 of 6 runs — the strongest consensus of the session. The stock is down ~25% YTD despite 15–18% revenue growth and a $37B AI run rate growing 123% YoY, leaving it at its cheapest forward multiple (~21x NTM earnings) since 2023. The July 28–29 earnings print is a binary re-rating event where Azure trajectory and FY2027 capex guidance are the swing variables; a defined-risk call spread is the preferred expression given potential 5–10% move in either direction.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 28–29, 2026 after close — Azure revenue growth, AI Foundry traction (14,000 customers), and FY2027 capex guidance disclosure
ideas.edge: Consensus analyst price target ~$589–$592 vs. ~$383 current price implies ~54–56% upside; forward P/E of ~21x is cheapest since 2023 despite $37B AI run rate growing 123% YoY and 97 analysts majority-rated Buy/Strong Buy
ideas.bull_case: Azure continues its $75B+ run rate with accelerating AI Foundry adoption, FY2027 capex guidance reassures on ROI discipline, and the stock re-rates toward the analyst consensus target of ~$590 — a 54%+ move from current levels.
ideas.bear_case: FY2027 capex guidance of $80–146B spooks markets on margin compression, Azure growth decelerates below expectations, and the stock's premium over broad market peers erodes further despite the valuation reset.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in all 6 overnight screens with high conviction in 5 of 6 runs, tied with MSFT for the top consensus slot. The stock trades at ~21.7x forward earnings — matching the broad S&P 500 — despite 85% YoY revenue growth, a record $75.2B data center quarter, and HBM supply sold out through 2027. Near-term re-rating catalysts arrive before the August 26 earnings print: TSMC on July 16 and hyperscaler prints (MSFT, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta) in late July confirm or deny the AI capex trajectory that underpins NVDA demand.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC earnings July 16 (sector proxy); MSFT/hyperscaler prints July 29–30 confirming AI capex; NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026 after close
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of 21.7x vs. 5-year average of 72x (Goldman Sachs); Cantor Fitzgerald $350 price target implies ~73% upside vs. current ~$202; hyperscaler AI capex projected to exceed $1T in 2027 vs. $650B in 2026
ideas.bull_case: Hyperscaler earnings in late July confirm $1T+ AI capex trajectory, TSMC July 16 data validates HBM demand, and NVDA re-rates toward analyst targets as investors recognize the S&P 500-equivalent valuation on category-dominant AI infrastructure growth.
ideas.bear_case: Any hyperscaler capex guidance reduction or China export restriction escalation cuts forward estimates sharply; at only 21.7x forward, there is limited multiple compression room but earnings risk is significant given high absolute expectations.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 overnight screens and debuts on Nasdaq today as the largest foreign ADR listing in history (~$28B), offering direct exposure to the world's #1 HBM supplier to NVIDIA. Priced at $149/ADS with pre-open indications already at ~$158 (+6.1%), the offering was 7x oversubscribed. The structural thesis is valuation re-rating: HSBC identifies a 35% 'Korea Discount' vs. Micron that the Nasdaq listing is designed to compress, while 2026 revenue projections of ~$235B represent more than 3x 2025 levels. All 6 screens flagged medium-or-higher conviction, but lack of options and first-day volatility argue for sizing up only after Monday's regular session opens under SKHY.
ideas.catalyst: IPO debut today July 10, 2026 — regular trading opens Monday July 13 under SKHY; secondary risk event July 29 when new Korean shares enter KOSPI circulation (potential dilution/selling pressure)
ideas.edge: 7x oversubscribed at $149; pre-open indicated at ~$158 (+6.1%); HSBC identifies 35% structural valuation discount vs. Micron that Nasdaq listing targets; HBM supply sold out through most of 2027 with NVDA as anchor customer
ideas.bull_case: Korea Discount compresses as global investors gain direct access, AI memory shortage pricing power drives 2026 revenue toward $235B, and SKHY trades to parity with Micron — implying substantial upside from IPO price.
ideas.bear_case: First-day euphoria fades, July 29 KOSPI dilution event creates selling pressure, and lack of options liquidity limits hedging — a break below the $149 IPO price could trigger stop-loss cascades in an illiquid new listing.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 5 of 6 overnight screens with mostly medium conviction, reflecting a clean but less asymmetric setup compared to MSFT and NVDA. The stock is up 8%+ in July alone heading into a July 30 fiscal Q3 print, with management guiding 14–17% revenue growth on top of Q2's 22% iPhone sales surge. A rumored 5-iPhone fall launch lineup including a ~$2,500 foldable adds product cycle optionality. At ~37x forward earnings — the richest valuation of the four consensus names — the risk/reward is more balanced, favoring defined-risk expressions like a bull call spread ahead of the print.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings July 30, 2026 — management guidance for 14–17% revenue growth; rumored foldable iPhone launch this fall as secondary re-rating catalyst
ideas.edge: Fiscal Q2 revenue $111.2B (+17% YoY) with 22% iPhone sales growth; stock up 8%+ in July with July 30 earnings approaching; within ~4% of overtaking NVDA as world's most valuable company at ~$4.5T market cap
ideas.bull_case: Q3 beats the 14–17% guidance range, services margin expansion continues, and foldable iPhone pre-launch buzz accelerates — AAPL reclaims the world's most valuable company title and extends July momentum.
ideas.bear_case: At 37x forward earnings, any guidance miss or macro softness from a hawkish FOMC on July 29 (one day prior) compresses the premium multiple sharply; AI monetization timeline remains less clear than MSFT or NVDA.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ options appeared in 3 of 6 overnight screens as the consensus macro hedge/directional vehicle for the coming two-week catalyst cluster. June CPI on July 14 is the pivotal pre-FOMC inflation read (May was 4.2% YoY with energy as primary driver; WTI crude has since pulled back to ~$71.57, suggesting potential relief), followed by the rare triple-catalyst convergence on July 29–30 when FOMC, MSFT earnings, and AAPL earnings all land within 24 hours. CME FedWatch shows 78% hold probability — a softer CPI print could spike QQQ 1.5–3% by July 18; a clean AI earnings sweep could push 3–5% in the July 29–30 window.
ideas.catalyst: June CPI July 14 at 8:30am ET (immediate catalyst); FOMC decision July 29 + MSFT earnings July 29 + AAPL earnings July 30 (triple-catalyst convergence)
ideas.edge: Market pricing only 22–25% probability of a July hike; WTI pullback to $71.57 suggests energy relief in June CPI; Nasdaq-100 rebounded 1.6% on July 9 from oversold levels; a dovish CPI surprise or clean AI earnings sweep historically produces 1.5–5% QQQ moves — expressible via defined-risk call spreads or straddles
ideas.bull_case: June CPI prints soft (energy relief visible), FOMC holds with dovish language, MSFT and AAPL both beat on July 29–30 — QQQ sees a 3–5% single-week move, with call spread or straddle capturing the move at defined risk.
ideas.bear_case: CPI surprises hot on July 14, raising July 29 hike probability materially; FOMC turns hawkish or MSFT capex guidance disappoints — QQQ gives back recent gains with the 10-year yield already at a 4-week high of 4.58%.
